layers.name: source
layers.summary: the upstream LaTeX notes, vendored into an editable copy with a provenance header

layers.name: concept
layers.summary: what is worth animating — a reviewed concept map per chapter

layers.name: script
layers.summary: narration, beats, and a runtime contract; the source of truth for timing

layers.name: scene
layers.summary: generated Manim code — one voiceover scene per beat

layers.name: build
layers.summary: render, assemble, and measure: one reviewable .mp4 per chapter

tools.name: assemble
tools.summary: Concatenate the per-scene .mp4 outputs of a chapter into one video.

tools.name: check_notation
tools.summary: Flag notation that violates the project convention.

tools.name: check_status
tools.summary: Enforce the human-review status gates (the pipeline's core promise).

tools.name: check_sync
tools.summary: Report whether the generated layers are in sync, by content hash.

tools.name: clean_cache
tools.summary: Prune manim's intermediate render cache from a project's media/ tree.

tools.name: doctor
tools.summary: Health-check the rendering pipeline before manim spins up.

tools.name: init_project
tools.summary: Bootstrap a draft project.yaml from upstream source(s).

tools.name: lint_scene
tools.summary: Geometric scene lint — flag undeclared bbox overlaps after each Scene.play().

tools.name: lint_style
tools.summary: Static style lint: the mechanizable STYLE_BOOK rules, checked at the source.

tools.name: measure_runtime
tools.summary: Write measured beat durations back into each chapter's script front matter.

tools.name: normalize_notation
tools.summary: Rewrite disallowed notation in the LOCAL working copies (sources/).

tools.name: render
tools.summary: Render the buildable chapters listed in project.yaml.

tools.name: scaffold
tools.summary: Emit the mechanical skeleton of a pipeline layer for one chapter.

tools.name: stamp_provenance
tools.summary: Stamp content-hash provenance into the markdown and scene layers.

tools.name: vendor_sources
tools.summary: Vendor each upstream source into an editable local working copy.
